description: Add a reusable test helper that verifies flow conservation, capacity bounds, and cost consistency on a solved instance.
commit: test: add checkSolution helper for invariant verification
steps: Create solution_check_test.go (suffix `_test.go` so it compiles only in tests; package mcf). Import `math`, `math/big`, `testing`, `github.com/holiman/uint256`.
Define `type testErrorf interface { Helper(); Errorf(format string, args ...interface{}) }` so both `*testing.T` and a recording stub satisfy it.

Implement `func checkSolution(t testErrorf, arcs []Arc, n, src, sink int, res Result)`. Invariants: (1) every arc.Flow is non-nil, Sign()>=0, Cmp(Capacity)<=0; (2) per-node inflow/outflow: for i != src,sink assert equal; for src assert outflow-inflow == res.TotalFlow; for sink assert inflow-outflow == res.TotalFlow; (3) best-effort cost: compute sum(Cost*Flow) in big.Int; if fits int64, require equality with res.TotalCost; else skip.

Self-tests: `TestCheckSolution_Valid` — 3-node path 0->1->2 both arcs Flow=5 Cap=10; Result{TotalFlow:NewInt(5), TotalCost:<sum>, Feasible:true}; pass *testing.T; assert t.Failed() remains false. `TestCheckSolution_CapacityViolation` — same graph but arc 0 Flow=20 > Cap=10; use a `recordingT` struct implementing testErrorf and counting Errorf calls; assert count > 0 after checkSolution returns.
acceptance_criteria: solution_check_test.go defines `testErrorf` interface and `checkSolution(testErrorf, []Arc, n, src, sink int, Result)`.
TestCheckSolution_Valid passes without recorded errors; TestCheckSolution_CapacityViolation triggers at least one Errorf on the recording stub.
